headline: Microsoft Commits $700 Million To Boost Poland's Cybersecurity Infrastructure As Part Of The 2023 Polish Data Center Project

headline: Elon Musk Points To Bill Clinton-Era Government Job Cuts Amid Tesla Showroom Protests
summary: Tesla Inc. CEO Elon Musk drew attention to historic government downsizing efforts On Monday, amid growing protests over his role in the President Donald Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency and recent Tesla showroom demonstrations.

headline: Uber Files Lawsuit Against DoorDash Over Anticompetitive Conduct: 'Underhanded Tactics Have Cost Uber Millions Of Dollars In Revenue'

headline: Xi Jinping Meets Tech Giants As Chinese Markets Rally On Private Sector Support (UPDATED)
summary: A potential meeting between Chinese President Xi Jinping and Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. co-founder Jack Ma this week may fuel further gains in China's technology stocks, which have been on a blistering rally.

summary: BYD has outlined plans to commence "demonstration use" of all-solid-state batteries by 2027, with a broader adoption anticipated post-2030.

headline: Will Markets Be Closed For Presidents Day? Here's What Investors Should Know

headline: Apple Supplier Foxconn's Efforts To Make iPhones In India Thwarted By Tech And Talent Exports: Report
summary: China's tightening restrictions on technology and talent exports are reportedly stalling Apple supplier Foxconn's expansion in India, reflecting Beijing's broader strategy to maintain control over critical supply chains amid escalating trade tensions.

headline: Apple Maps May Introduce Ads Similar To Google Maps As iPhone Maker Explores New Revenue Stream, Says Top Analyst
summary: Apple is reportedly considering introducing ads to Apple Maps, similar to Google's paid search model, as part of its broader strategy to expand services revenue and capitalize on its growing user base.

headline: Ross Gerber Says He's 'Eventually' Selling His Cybertruck Because The 'Hate Is Real' As Protests Rock Tesla Showrooms
summary: Ross Gerber, CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management, announced Sunday he intends to "eventually" sell his Cybertruck, citing intense public hostility toward the Tesla Inc. brand.

headline: Intel Could Be Split In Two As Broadcom And TSMC Explore Deals, Trump White House Resists Foreign Ownership: Report
summary: Broadcom and TSMC are reportedly considering separate deals that could lead to Intel's breakup, while the Trump administration weighs in on the potential foreign control of its chip manufacturing operations.

headline: Jeff Bezos-Owned Washington Post Turns Down Ad Campaign That Asked, 'Who's Running This Country: Donald Trump Or Elon Musk?'
summary: The Washington Post rejected a $115,000 "Fire Elon Musk" ad campaign by Common Cause, which was set to run on Tuesday.
